meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Stop Debugging, Start Building
content.seo_description: Master project management to overcome debugging roadblocks. A beginner tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your first real-world app.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it's about systematic project management. By breaking features into atomic tasks and using AI-driven CLI tools like Claude Code and Antigravity, you move from 'stuck' to 'solved.' Structure your workflow to isolate logic, track state changes, and treat every error as a data-driven optimization task.
content.intro:
The screen is glowing, the cursor is blinking, and your terminal is screaming a wall of red text. You feel that familiar, sinking weight in your chest—the 'debugging wall.' It’s the invisible barrier between you and your first production-ready application, and it has stopped thousands of developers before they even began.

But here is the industry secret: elite engineers don't debug by guessing; they debug by managing their project architecture. By treating your code as a series of manageable, modular, and trackable systems, you turn chaos into a repeatable process. You are about to learn how to master your workflow, reclaim your focus, and finally finish the project that will define your portfolio.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Scope
steps.description: Stop trying to debug the whole app. Break your project into tiny, functional units to isolate the failure point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project structure and break the current feature into atomic tasks that I can execute sequentially to isolate the bug.

steps.title: Establish a Reproducible Test
steps.description: You cannot fix what you cannot reproduce. Create a minimal test case that triggers the bug consistently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a minimal unit test script that reproduces the current error state while ignoring unrelated UI logic.

steps.title: Instrument the Data Flow
steps.description: Trace the variable state through your functions to see exactly where the logic diverges from your expectations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Insert non-intrusive logging into these specific functions to trace the variable state flow during execution.

steps.title: Execute Targeted Refactoring
steps.description: Use AI to suggest a fix that maintains the structural integrity of your code without introducing regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the logs and suggest a refactor that fixes the state mismatch without changing the core function signature.

steps.title: Automate Future Guardrails
steps.description: Turn your bug fix into a permanent test case so this error never resurfaces in your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert the current fix into a persistent integration test to ensure this bug doesn't reappear during future commits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message='fix: resolved state drift and added regression test'

pro_tips: Always commit your 'broken' state to a separate branch before attempting a fix; it allows for a true reset if your debugging leads you further into the weeds.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code by explaining the logic out loud before running the command; the act of explaining often reveals the flaw.
If you find yourself spending more than 30 minutes on a single bug, stop coding and map the state flow on paper—visualizing data usually reveals the architecture error.
Treat your error messages as leads, not insults; copy the stack trace directly into your context window to let the AI map the path to the source.
Prioritize 'clean code' over 'clever code' during the debugging phase; complex one-liners are usually where the logic bugs hide.
content.next_steps: Once you have resolved your current bug, document the fix in your project README under a 'Troubleshooting' section. This builds your reputation as a developer who doesn't just write code, but maintains it. Then, push your branch to GitHub to solidify your progress in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the framework to stop fearing bugs and start mastering your craft. Every error is just a lesson in disguise, and you have the tools to turn those lessons into professional-grade software. The world needs your unique perspective and your build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Stop trying to debug the whole app. Break your project into tiny, functional units to isolate the failure point.
You cannot fix what you cannot reproduce. Create a minimal test case that triggers the bug consistently.
Trace the variable state through your functions to see exactly where the logic diverges from your expectations.
Use AI to suggest a fix that maintains the structural integrity of your code without introducing regression.
Turn your bug fix into a permanent test case so this error never resurfaces in your project.
